channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tiffany & Co showcases exclusive High Jewellery collection in New York
item.description: Last month, Tiffany & Co hosted an extravagant event in a 19th-century Upper East Side building in New York to unveil its Hidden Garden High Jewellery collection. The venue itself was a nod to history...

item.title: Kayole Boy Who Looked After Siblings Gets Full Scholarship to Visa Oshwal Academy
item.description: In Nairobi, Tito Wambua, a young boy from Kayole, has captured the hearts of many Kenyans after stepping up to care for his four siblings without much help. With his mother facing health challenges an...

item.title: Maharashtra Court Convicts BJP Minister Nitesh Rane for Assaulting Civic Engineer
item.description: In a notable judgment from Maharashtra, Sindhudurg Additional Sessions Court on Monday found BJP minister Nitesh Rane guilty of assaulting a civic engineer and preventing him from performing official ...

item.title: Ongoing Impeachment Hearing of VP Sara Duterte on April 29, 2026
item.description: On April 29, 2026, the House Committee on Justice commenced the impeachment hearing of Vice President Sara Duterte. The event is being closely followed by both the media and the public due to its poli...

item.title: Arewa Forum Says No New Vehicle Tax, Advises Nigerians to Verify Official Sources
item.description: Di Arewa Socio-Economic Forum don come out make e clear say di talk wey dey go round for social media about new vehicle tax na lie. Dem don check and confirm say neither di Nigeria Revenue Service (NR...

item.title: Three Accused of Killing Seven Monswamy Family Members Withdraw Bail in Court
item.description: Three men, Linda Blessing Mthiyane (28), Bongumenzi Mpungose (26), and Mthandeni Luyanda Mthiyane (21), appeared in the KwaDukuza Magistrate’s Court facing 14 charges, including seven counts of murder...

item.title: Public Servants to Receive 4% Wage Increase from 1 April 2026
item.description: The Department of Public Service and Administration, under Minister Inkosi Mzamo Buthelezi, has confirmed a 4% wage increase for public servants on salary levels 1 to 12, effective from 1 April 2026. ...

item.title: CIF CEO Highlights Ethiopia's Leadership in Climate Action and Financing
item.description: Ethiopia has been recognized for its proactive leadership in addressing climate change both regionally and globally, according to Tariye Gbadegesin, Chief Executive Officer of the Climate Investment F...

item.title: US Government to Release Limited-Edition Passports Featuring Trump for 250th Independence Anniversary
item.description: Di US government don announce say dem go release limited-edition passports wey go get President Donald Trump picture on top, to celebrate di 250th anniversary of di US Declaration of Independence. Di ...

item.title: Niesr warns UK economy may slow and borrowing could rise due to Iran conflict
item.description: The UK is confronting a potential £35 billion economic impact and the risk of a recession this year as a result of the ongoing conflict in Iran, according to the National Institute of Economic and Soc...

item.title: Journalistic Focus on Liberal Leaders Overshadows Policy Debate
item.description: During a recent episode of ABC’s Insiders, Liberal leader Angus Taylor faced questions from David Speers regarding the party’s controversial preference for One Nation in the Farrer electorate. The int...

item.title: Family in Mahogany shaken by near-collision on local street
item.description: Residents of Calgary's Mahogany neighbourhood were left deeply unsettled after a vehicle nearly collided with a family’s home, causing significant distress in the community. Witnesses reported that th...

item.title: Kerala Dalit Groups Protest Student Suicide, Demand Justice
item.description: On Tuesday, several Dalit organisations in Kerala called for a dawn-to-dusk bandh protesting the suicide of a Dalit dental student at a university in Kannur. The protest, which demanded justice for th...

item.title: Park Bo-young Explores Crime Genre with New Series ‘Gold Land’
item.description: South Korean actress Park Bo-young is making a bold shift in her acting career by entering the crime genre with the upcoming series 'Gold Land.' Known for her versatile performances in romantic and co...

item.title: Rising Demand for Pre-Owned Goods Boosts Hustle Opportunities in Kenya
item.description: In Kenya, the appetite for second-hand goods has shot up by 70% in the first quarter of 2026 compared to the same period last year, according to data from Jiji, Africa’s leading online classifieds pla...

item.title: UK population growth to slow with deaths surpassing births from 2026
item.description: The Office for National Statistics (ONS) has projected that, beginning in 2026, deaths will consistently outnumber births in the UK, marking a significant demographic shift. Population growth over the...

item.title: UK Identified as Most Non-Compliant with Global Nuclear Weapons Ban
item.description: The United Kingdom has been ranked as the worst violator of the Treaty on the Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons (TPNW) in the 2026 Nuclear Weapons Ban Monitor report by Norwegian People's Aid. The report...

item.title: US Justice Department charges former FBI director James Comey over Instagram post of seashells arranged as '86 47'
item.description: The US Justice Department, under acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, has filed criminal charges against former FBI director James Comey for allegedly threatening the life of President Donald Trump. ...

item.title: Key Considerations Before Committing to Expensive Private School Fees
item.description: In this article, financial adviser Sangram Rana discusses the important questions families should ask before enrolling their children in expensive private schools. Using a recent example of a couple f...

item.title: High Court rules transgenders cannot claim traditional congratulatory gifts
item.description: The Delhi High Court has clarified the legal position regarding the entitlement of transgender persons to traditional 'badhai', which are customary gifts or recognitions given during auspicious occasi...
